methods.description: Criteria and process for selecting patients for the study based on clinical symptoms.

steps.description: We included in the study any patient aged 5 years and more, with clinical symptoms suggesting asthma and/or rhinitis and/or conjunctivitis and/or cutaneous manifestations such as eczema or urticaria.
steps.searchable_summary: select patients aged 5+ with specific clinical symptoms

methods.description: Procedure for conducting a prick test using allergens to diagnose allergic reactions.

steps.description: Realization of a prick test (PT) on 7 allergens provided by Stallergenes Laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct prick test using specified allergens

steps.description: We used the histamine dihydrochloride (10 mg/ml) as a positive control to ensure skin reactivity and glycerol containing the diluent of the prick test solution as a negative control to eliminate dermographism.
steps.searchable_summary: use positive and negative controls for prick test validation

steps.description: We considered a test positive if the wheal diameter obtained was 3 mm greater than the negative control and/or 50% greater than the diameter of the positive control.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze prick test results for positive reaction

methods.description: Statistical analysis of the collected data using Epi Info software and Chi-2 test.

steps.description: Data analysis was performed by the software Epi Info version 6.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze data using Epi Info version 6

steps.description: Statistical comparisons were made by the Chi-2 test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical comparisons using Chi-2 test
